title: viola adunca var. bellidifolia
intro: Viola adunca var. bellidifolia, also known as the pansy violet or Bellflower violet, is a rare and endangered variety of the Viola adunca plant species, native to the Pacific Northwest region of North America. This plant is valued for its medicinal properties, which include its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, making it a potential remedy for skin conditions and wounds. The health benefits of Viola adunca var. bellidifolia include its ability to soothe and calm digestive issues, as well as its potential to reduce stress and anxiety. Herbal preparations of the plant can be made into teas, infusions, and salves, and it is often used in traditional medicine and herbal remedies.
